prompt:
I got a HardFault on my STM32F4 and I captured these register values:
CFSR = 0x00008200
HFSR = 0x40000000
MMFAR = 0x00000010
BFAR = 0x00000000
The fault happens randomly, sometimes after 2 minutes, sometimes after 30 minutes. The PC from the exception frame points to a memcpy inside my CAN message parser. Help me debug this.

assertions.name: decodes_cfsr_as_busfault
assertions.description: Correctly decodes CFSR=0x00008200 as a BusFault (PRECISERR + BFARVALID in BFSR byte [15:8]), NOT as a MemManage fault.

assertions.name: identifies_null_pointer
assertions.description: Identifies that BFAR=0x00000000 indicates a null pointer dereference

assertions.name: notes_mmfar_stale
assertions.description: Notes that MMFAR=0x10 is stale because MMARVALID (MMFSR bit 7) is NOT set

assertions.name: recommends_debugging_steps
assertions.description: Provides actionable debugging steps (GDB watchpoint, ASan, or code review)

assertions.name: explains_intermittent_nature
assertions.description: Explains why the fault is intermittent (race condition, timing-dependent buffer state)
